channel.title: Cool Papers — Rendeiro Lab
channel.description: Recent interesting papers shared by the Rendeiro Lab team

item.title: The Human Pleiotropic Map of GWAS Associations and Therapeutic Implications
item.author: Ariadna Villanueva Marijuan
item.description: [bioRxiv] genome-wide target prioritization

item.title: Thousandfold Expansion Microscopy
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [bioRxiv] Slightly off topic, but maybe we can soon study sub cellular structures by eye or cheap microscopes?

item.title: Cophenetic Spatial Topology Embedding reveals multiscale tissue architecture in spatial omics
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [bioRxiv]

item.title: Pan-cancer spatial atlas of tertiary lymphoid structures
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Science]

item.title: MORPHE: Bridging Image Generation and Spatial Omics for Tissue Synthesis
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [bioRxiv]

item.title: Universal transcriptomic hallmarks of mammalian ageing and mortality
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [Nature]

item.title: Spatiotemporal transcriptome atlas of human embryos after gastrulation
item.author: Yimin Zheng
item.description: [Nature] Typically BGI work

item.title: TopoSlide: Topologically-Informed Histopathology Whole Slide Image Representation Learning
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [CVF Open Access] Might be useful for Simon & Shrestha

item.title: A Spatial Proteomic Atlas of Tertiary Lymphoid Structures in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer Identifies a Novel Predictive Class of Lymphoid Aggregates
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [bioRxiv]

item.title: A deep-learning framework reveals whole-body perturbations at cell level
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [Nature]

item.title: Multiplexed magnetic resonance imaging
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature] the latest in cutting-edge MRI

item.title: Sleep chart of biological ageing clocks in middle and late life
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature] sleep clocks! Strange paper but interesting phenotypes and genetics @Ariadna Villanueva Marijuan

item.title: A Spatial Proteomic Atlas of Tertiary Lymphoid Structures in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer Identifies a Novel Predictive Class of Lymphoid Aggregates
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [bioRxiv]

item.title: Plasma proteomic signature of frailty in 50,506 adults - ScienceDirect
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Cell Press] Plasma proteomic signature of frailty in 50,506 adults

item.title: Hidden immune memory niches in inflammatory skin diseases
item.author: Lisa Kleissl
item.description: [bioRxiv] The study links histopathology and atlas-scale genomics to reveal novel insights into inflammatory disease pathogenesis, chronicity, and potentially curative therapeutic avenues, using skin as an exemplar tissue for this approach

item.title: sc-ChromAging: A Single-Cell Chromatin Accessibility-based Clock Decodes Cell-Type-Specific Epigenetic Aging Trajectories
item.author: Lisa Kleissl
item.description: [npj Aging] sc-ChromAging, a chromatin accessibility-based aging clock, was developed using single-cell ATACseq from 401 Chinese individuals.

item.title: Non-invasive profiling of the tumour microenvironment with spatial ecotypes
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [Nature] Quite interesting study where they find conserved spatial ecotypes (spatially dependent cell states and multicellular ecosystems) in spatial transcriptomics data, which are linked to immune therapy response. They can even recover the spatial ecotypes' methylation profiles from plasma cDNA.

item.title: SPARC: A mechanism-aware spatial representation from routine histology predicts cancer survival and therapy response
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [medRxiv]

item.title: IBDome: An integrated molecular, histopathological, and clinical atlas of inflammatory bowel diseases
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [bioRxiv] in case we didn't talk about it before - this is a great resource that may be of use to you. I know the last author so we could ask for data before it's accepted.

item.title: How to design effective scientific figures
item.author: Yimin Zheng
item.description: [Nature Human Behaviour] A nice little article on Nature human behavior discussing general ideas on how to design scientific figures.

item.title: OptimusKG: Unifying biomedical knowledge in a modern multimodal graph
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [arXiv] finally an easy way to load the Zitnik lab Knowledge-graph! import optimuskg nodes, edges = optimuskg.load_graph(lcc=True)

item.title: Linking spatial biology and clinical histology via Haiku
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [arXiv] �A tri-modal contrastive learning model trained on multiplexed immunofluorescence (mIF), H&E and clinical metadata from 11 organs of 1,606 patients. Both code and model weights are available: Github: Model weights:

item.title: Pan-cancer virtual spatial transcriptomics from routine histology with Phoenix
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [bioRxiv] spatial gene expression prediction from H&E via flow matching. Weights not available yet. Full TCGA dataset with inference will be available.

item.title: AI framework for multidisease detection via retinal imaging
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature Medicine] the latest in ocular AI with implications for multi-organ health prediction - note this not OCT but color fundus photography - a much simpler, faster (30sec) and cheaper imaging method

item.title: Multimodal data analysis reveals asynchronous aging dynamics across female reproductive organs
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature Aging] the published version of - would be good to have a close look and see what resources we can use

item.title: An agentic framework for autonomous scientific discovery in cancer pathology
item.author: Yimin Zheng
item.description: [Nature Medicine]

item.title: VitaminP: cross-modal learning enables whole-cell segmentation from routine histology
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [arXiv] Nuclear and cell segmentation by using a cross-modal framework and transferring molecular boundary information from mIF and detect cytoplasmic contrast in H&E images. They also provide their models, the models seem to be straight forward to use:

item.title: A 3D morphogenetic blueprint for metastatic outgrowth in breast cancer
item.author: Yimin Zheng
item.description: [Cell] Basically, they found that the metastatic potential of breast cancer is highly correlated with the 3D architecture

item.title: Dynamics of genetic and somatic trade-offs in ageing and mortality
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature]

item.title: Weighted Genetic Risk Scores and Prediction of Weight Gain in Solid Organ Transplant Populations
item.author: Ariadna Villanueva Marijuan
item.description: [PLOS ONE] Here they describe a more or less similar approach to what I proposed when creating a PGS with a very small population. Three GRS were built following a weighted GRS (w-GRS) method as previously described [28] with 32 SNPs (SNP group#1) and 97 SNPs (SNP group#2) both from GWAS, and 19 SNPs (SNP group#3) from candidate genes. Briefly, genotypes from each SNP were coded as 0, 1 or 2 according to the number of BMI risk alleles and each polymorphism was then weighted by its �-coefficient (allele eff

item.title: A spatial atlas of the healthy human liver from live donors
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature]

item.title: 95% of Experimental Life-Science Papers in Three Months of Nature Used Hypothesis Testing Without Justifying Sample Sizes
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: a so(m)ber assessment of statistical testing use in life sciences. To me the idea that life science research is largely designed to discover the hypothesis, not test pre-specified ones and its implications is the take-home message here.

item.title: Tissue morphology predicts telomere shortening in human tissues
item.author: Yimin Zheng
item.description: [Cell Reports Methods] Instead of predicting chronological age, they just predict telomere length, an interesting study.

item.title: Whole organism 3D mapping reveals universal branching topology and biophysical optimization governs vascular and nervous system development
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [bioRxiv] A new paper from the Wirtz lab where they developed a computational pipeline for whole-organism 3D imaging (z stacked H&E) to reconstruct the complete vascular and nervous systems of rhesus macaque, mouse, and turtle embryos. Data is apparently available upon request.

item.title: Understanding multicellular function and disease with human tissue-specific networks
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature Genetics] an older method which was added to HumanBase which has something that could be of interest to us, in particular Tamas and Ariadna: NetWAS - Network-wide Association Study — HumanBase 1.0 documentation "NetWAS trains a support vector machine classifier using nominally significant (P < 0.01) genes as positive examples and 10,000 randomly selected non-significant (P ≥ 0.01) genes as negatives. The classifier is constructed using a tissue network relevant to a disease (e.g. kidney for hypertension),

item.title: annbatch unlocks terabyte-scale training of biological data in anndata
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [arXiv]

item.title: PRET is a few-shot system for pan-cancer recognition without example training
item.author: Yimin Zheng
item.description: [Nature Cancer] A way to build clinical AI without training.

item.title: scAgeClock: a single-cell transcriptome-based human aging clock model using gated multi-head attention neural networks
item.author: Lisa Kleissl
item.description: [npj Aging] Not really a paper rather a publicly available model for network-based single-cell aging clock model. Maybe interesting for someone. Lisa

item.title: Generative machine learning unlocks the first proteome-wide image of human cells
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [bioRxiv]

item.title: tracel-ai/burn: a next generation tensor library and Deep Learning Framework
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: the next big thing on model training and inference? for those interested in rust, there's also a nice image algorithm library: see a full discussion on adopting these technologies for biological imaging here:

item.title: p16High-expressing immune cells control disease tolerance as a defense and health span-extending strategy
item.author: Lisa Kleissl
item.description: [Immunity]

item.title: MIPHEI-ViT: Multiplex Immunofluorescence Prediction from H&E Images using ViT Foundation Models
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [arXiv] a multiplex imaging prediction model from Sanofi

item.title: Functional hierarchy of the human neocortex across the lifespan
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature] relevant for the VITA dataset

item.title: CytoSyn: a Foundation Diffusion Model for Histopathology -- Tech Report
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [arXiv] the write-up of Owkin's generative model

item.title: MOOZY: A Patient-First Foundation Model for Computational Pathology
item.author: Andre Rendeiro

item.title: Toward Computationally Complete Spatial Omics
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [bioRxiv] A new framework to integrate histology, epigenome, transcriptome, proteome, and metabolome into a unified representation.

item.title: Mixture of Mini Experts: Overcoming the Linear Layer Bottleneck in Multiple Instance Learning
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: latest from the Mahmood lab

item.title: Reimagining systematic anatomy: The conclusion of the quartet
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Clinical Anatomy] Reimagining systematic anatomy: The conclusion of the quartet - Neumann - 2022 - Clinical Anatomy - Wiley Online Library

item.title: Single-cell atlas of human lung aging identifies cell type dyssynchrony and increased transcriptional entropy
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature Communications] potentially useful dataset to validate insights from lung tissue aging

item.title: Hidden immune memory niches in inflammatory skin diseases
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [bioRxiv] Another interesting Skin paper, in which they profile 113 Xenium skin sections together with histology images for healthy, atopic dermatitis and psoriasis - likely relevant for @Lisa Kleissl

item.title: Single-cell spatial transcriptomic analysis of human skin anatomy
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [Nature Genetics] Potentially a nice dataset, where they also study tissue units/ neighborhoods in the skin in healthy, pathology, but also age. They find e.g., a stromal unit decreasing with age and an immune-poor perivascular niche increasing with age. They also use GTEx bulk RNA-seq, but so far not the images. MERFISH and histology image data is available at:

item.title: Adapting a Pre-trained Single-Cell Foundation Model to Spatial Gene Expression Generation from Histology Images
item.author: Clemens Kohl
item.description: [arXiv] Interesting method that allows for retrofitting of foundation models to another modality.

item.title: Thymic health consequences in adults
item.author: Lisa Kleissl
item.description: [Nature] "We developed a d eep learning framework to quantify thymic health from routine radiographic images and evaluated its association with longevity and risk of major age-associated diseases in two large prospective cohorts of asymptomatic adults: the National Lung Screening Trial ( = 25,031) and the Framingham Heart Study (

item.title: Whole-organ and whole-body 3D atlases enable cellome-wide profiling
item.author: Yimin Zheng
item.description: [Cell]

item.title: HistoAtlas: A Pan-Cancer Morphology Atlas Linking Histomics to Molecular Programs and Clinical Outcomes
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [arXiv] A solo paper by one of the software developers of HistoPlus (Pierre, who we also met with for HistoPlus).

item.title: HistoGWAS: An AI Framework for Automated and Interpretable Genetic Analysis of Tissue Phenotypes
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [bioRxiv]

item.title: Intestinal interoceptive dysfunction drives age-associated cognitive decline
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature] particularly relevant in the context of the CeMM 'flagship' project - let's have a look so we are familiar with the scientific language, methods, ideas

item.title: Ageing promotes metastasis via activation of the integrated stress response
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature]

item.title: Simultaneous spatial transcriptomics and morphology profiling as tools to explore how microglia change with age
item.author: Lisa Kleissl
item.description: [Nature Aging]

item.title: imAgeScore, a Cell Painting-Based Predictor of Cellular Age for High-throughput Drug Screening Applications
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [bioRxiv] Scoring of primary human dermal fibroblasts with Cell Painting. imAgeScore correlates with chronological and DNA methylation-based age estimates and captures coordinated morphological changes across nuclear and cytoplasmic compartments."

item.title: Toward Computationally Complete Spatial Omics
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [bioRxiv]

item.title: Estropausal gut microbiota transplant improves measures of ovarian function in adult mice
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature Aging] interesting study with lots of valuable resources for ovarian aging and histology - particularly relevant to Parijat

item.title: TransBrain: a computational framework for translating brain-wide phenotypes between humans and mice
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature Methods] sounds cool and probably a lot to learn here

item.title: Ten mouse organs proteome and metabolome atlas from adult to aging
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Genome Medicine] if we ever want a dataset of mouse proteomics during aging

item.title: MuViT: Multi-Resolution Vision Transformers for Learning Across Scales in Microscopy
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [arXiv] model for microscopy data where some ideas may be useful to us

item.title: Confounding factors and biases abound when predicting molecular biomarkers from histological images
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature Biomedical Engineering]

item.title: Foundation model for cancer imaging biomarkers
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature Machine Intelligence] good to keep an eye on the radiology side of things

item.title: GrapHist: Graph Self-Supervised Learning for Histopathology
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [arXiv] cool work on graph-based learning - we could try it out already in basically every project in which we learn slide labels; especially relevant for Shrestha, Simon

item.title: 3D-Guided Scalable Flow Matching for Generating Volumetric Tissue Spatial Transcriptomics from Serial Histology
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [arXiv]

item.title: Revisiting the blueprint for an interpretable virtual cell
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature Reviews Genetics] important historical insights, and a provoking thought we should all be having

item.title: Cellular Aging Signatures in the Plasma Proteome 2 Record Human Health and Disease
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [bioRxiv] New paper from the Wyss-Coray lab.

item.title: Towards Spatial Transcriptomics-driven Pathology Foundation Models
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [arXiv] latest from Mahmood lab - would be good to run inference for a small set of GTEx slides and benchmark how aggregation recovers the bulk RNA-seq profiles

item.title: Deep Label Distribution Learning for Leveraging Image Ambiguity
item.author: SSrivastava
item.description: Converts the hard labels into a distribution and then calculates loss between prediction probability and ground truth probabilities, maybe we can use this to solve the imbalance in age in the GTEx dataset.

item.title: Circulating Cell Type Senescence Signatures Reveal High-Resolution Health Status and 2 Trajectories in Human Longitudinal Studies
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [medRxiv] Circulating Cell Type Senescence Signatures Reveal High-Resolution Health Status and 2 Trajectories in Human Longitudinal Studies

item.title: A lightweight, ultrafast and general embedding framework for large-scale spatial omics data
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [bioRxiv] very impressive work, we should study it (method and paper); maybe particularly relevant for Simon regarding previous foundation model ideas

item.title: AtlasPatch: An Efficient and Scalable Tool for Whole Slide Image Preprocessing in Computational Pathology
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [arXiv] maybe another Grandqc competitor. They also have or plan a patch writing database functionality:

item.title: Unifying the genetic landscape of common and rare diseases via latent neighborhoods
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [bioRxiv]

item.title: HistoSweep enables cellular-resolution tissue quality control for gigapixel images in digital pathology and spatial omics
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [bioRxiv] looks like there is a competitor to GrandQC

item.title: PaperBanana: Automating Academic Illustration for AI Scientists
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [arXiv]

item.title: An atlas of human histological diversity
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [bioRxiv] A new very relevant study on the GTEx dataset, associating "morphemes" (histology signatures) with the patho-clinical and multi-omics data, to reveal influences of age, sex, genetics, and pathologies. Probably relevant for everybody, but especially for the slide hub project and @Ariadna Villanueva Marijuan

item.title: Heritability of intrinsic human life span is about 50% when confounding factors are addressed
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Science] commentary also here:

item.title: VISTA-PATH: An interactive foundation model for pathology image segmentation and quantitative analysis in computational pathology
item.author: Samir Moustafa
item.description: [arXiv] interactive pathology segmentation foundation model combining vision, language, and expert prompts, strong gains on complex tissue segmentation and useful for clinically grounded benchmarks

item.title: STPath: a generative foundation model for integrating spatial transcriptomics and whole-slide images
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [npj Digital Medicine] another ST-generating models from H&E; would be good to add to LazySlide

item.title: CELLama: Foundation Model for Single Cell and Spatial Transcriptomics by Cell Embedding Leveraging Language Model Abilities
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Advanced Science] interesting model combining ST and language; would be good to add to LazySlide

item.title: A Foundational Generative Model for Cross-platform Unified Enhancement of Spatial Transcriptomics
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: a model for ST prediction from histology; potentially useful paper to understand how to benchmark/validate these types of models; when available would be good to include in LazySlide

item.title: A multimodal sleep foundation model for disease prediction
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature Medicine]

item.title: A foundation model for continuous glucose monitoring data
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature]

item.title: AI-enabled virtual spatial proteomics from histopathology for interpretable biomarker discovery in lung cancer
item.author: Ernesto Abila
item.description: [Nature Medicine]

item.title: HEDeST: An Integrative Approach to Enhance Spatial Transcriptomic Deconvolution with Histology
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [bioRxiv]

item.title: Combining Graph Neural Network and Mamba to Capture Local and Global Tissue Spatial Relationships in Whole Slide Images
item.author: SSrivastava
item.description: [arXiv] This paper uses Mamba a specific type of state space model (SSM) (these are proposed to be faster and efficient alternative for the attention mechanism). SSM combines the fast nature of RNNs during inference along with the complexity of transformers during training.

item.title: Advancing single-cell omics and cell-based therapeutics with quantum computing
item.author: Yimin Zheng
item.description: [Nature Reviews Molecular Cell Biology]

item.title: High-parameter spatial multi-omics through histology-anchored integration
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature Methods]

item.title: A full life cycle biological clock based on routine clinical data and its impact in health and diseases
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [Nature Medicine] pretty cool work that flew a bit more under the radar than the other paper on EHR data published in Nature (also more centered on aging)

item.title: SpaCEy: Discovery of Functional Spatial Tissue Patterns by Association with Clinical Features Using Explainable Graph Neural Networks
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [bioRxiv]

item.title: Single-cell phenomics through integrated imaging and molecular profiling
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [bioRxiv] a cool single-cell sequencing method where each cell transcriptome is paired to its own image

item.title: Digital Spatial Pathway Mapping Reveals Prognostic Tumor States in Head and Neck Cancer
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [bioRxiv] looks like nice work - very relevant to Tamas but also everyone else

item.title: PULSAR: a Foundation Model for Multi-scale and Multicellular Biology
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [bioRxiv]

item.title: Multi-tissue spatial transcriptomics reveals biological age hotspots in mouse and human aging
item.author: Andre Rendeiro
item.description: [bioRxiv] let's have a close look

item.title: NOVA: An Agentic Framework for Automated Histopathology Analysis and Discovery
item.author: Yimin Zheng
item.description: [arXiv] An agent developed by Harvard and Microsoft research. LazySlide is used as a backend 😁

item.title: A deep learning-based multiscale integration of spatial omics with tumor morphology
item.author: Yimin Zheng
item.description: [Nature Communications] Another model predicting transcriptome from HE slides, by Owkin.
